channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wild weather blamed for deaths of 3 kids in Michigan and man in an Amish buggy in Indiana
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — A tree fell on a van in Michigan, killing three children, during a fierce weekend storm that swept the region and contributed to the death of an 84-year-old man in an Amish buggy in Indiana, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic base's anger puts some party leaders on shaky ground
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Democratic base is angry. Not just at President Donald Trump, Elon Musk and the “Make America Great Again” movement. Rank-and-file Democrats are mad at their own leaders and increasingly agitating to replace them. Democrats in Arizona and Georgia pushed out their party chairs.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams to discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams are expected to speak to the media in their first news conference since returning to Earth on March 18.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: Strong winds are driving an out-of-control wildfire through a remote area of eastern California, prompting evacuations for hundreds of homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oliver Stone to testify at House hearing on JFK files
item.description: American filmmaker Oliver Stone — whose political thriller, “JFK,” made waves more than 30 years ago — will testify on Tuesday before a House committee on the release of new documents related to the assassination of John F. Kennedy Jr., according to a press release from the committee.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Lawyers for 'morbidly obese' Florida death row inmate argue his weight could cause botched lethal injection
item.description: Lawyers for a Florida man on death row are seeking to halt his execution set for next week, arguing that his obesity could lead to a botched lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month, NBC News reported. The trio were discovered deceased on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge weighs request to withhold investigation records in deaths of Gene Hackman and wife
item.description: A New Mexico court is weighing whether to block the disclosure of an array of records from an investigation into the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and wife, Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US armored vehicle pulled from Lithuanian swamp, fate of 4 soldiers unknown
item.description: A U.S. M88 Hercules armored recovery vehicle was retrieved in Pabradė, Lithuania after sinking last week while carrying four American soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Denny Hamlin ends 10-year win drought at Martinsville Speedway
item.description: MARTINSVILLE, Va. (AP) — At the track he loves to dominate, Denny Hamlin was back on top with a new face atop his pit box.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Didn't know Delta had trading cards? Many others didn't, either, until at least one TikTok user showcased his collection in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Tsunami warning lifted after 7.0 earthquake near Tonga in the South Pacific
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A strong 7.0 magnitude earthquake hit near Tonga, prompting an initial tsunami warning that was later lifted for the Pacific island country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, WGN 9

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: ICE detains University of Minnesota international graduate student near Twin Cities campus
item.description: An foreign grad student attending the University of Minnesota has been detained by ICE. The DHS said the student had a prior criminal history for a DUI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Statham’s 'A Working Man’ upsets ‘Snow White’ to take No. 1 at the box office
item.description: In an unexpected upset, the Jason Statham thriller “A Working Man” took No. 1 at the box office, besting the rapidly declining “Snow White,” according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Thousands of people have no power in Wisconsin, Michigan and Indiana after freezing rain or severe storms brought down trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, ABC 4

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: The Long Island iced tea may not be from that Long Island
item.description: There's likely a very specific Long Island you think of when you hear "Long Island iced tea." You may, however, be wrong.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration to cut California school funding over LGBT+ policies
item.description: California Senator Scott Wiener condemned Trump's decision to cut school funding in response to the state's LGBTQ+ policies in schools, stating that it is a form of cruelty towards LGBTQ kids and a distraction from Trump's own staff's security breach.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, Fox 5

item.title: Army Strykers deployed along Mexican border
item.description: With an array of electronic sensors, radar and infrared equipment, the crew of a newly deployed M1126 Stryker vehicle can detect movement even farther day or night and instantly communicate it to other Army units or U.S. Border Patrol agents.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC
